_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-23 Vascular Access/IV Fluid (Revised 3/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Vascular Access and IV Fluid Administration
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-23: Vascular Access/IV Fluid — MDHHS Approved 3/17/2025
purpose: Specialist-level skill. Governs peripheral IV, IO, and external jugular (EJ) vascular access and IV fluid administration. Existing central lines, PICC lines, and AV fistulas must NOT be accessed without Medical Control authorization.

sections.title: Peripheral IV Access
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-23: Vascular Access — Peripheral IV
points: Specialist and Paramedic
Do NOT access existing ports, PICC lines, central venous catheters, or AV fistulas without Medical Control authorization
Maximum attempts: 2 attempts per provider; maximum 4 total attempts across all providers
After 2 failed IV attempts in a critical patient: consider IO access
Needle sizes: adult standard 18–20 gauge; adult shock/arrest 14–18 gauge; pediatric 20–24 gauge
Saline lock (INT) preferred for patients not requiring continuous fluid administration
Macro drip tubing preferred for fluid administration

sections.title: Intraosseous (IO) Access
points: Specialist and Paramedic
CONSIDER IO EARLY — do not delay in critical patients
IO INDICATIONS: cardiac arrest; severe burn with hemodynamic compromise; shock; severe multi-system trauma with shock
PREFERRED SITE — ADULTS: proximal humerus (preferred in cardiac arrest — allows proximal delivery); proximal tibia
PREFERRED SITE — PEDIATRIC < 6 years: proximal tibia
After 2 failed IO attempts: contact Medical Control
LIDOCAINE 2% IO for CONSCIOUS patients to reduce infusion pain:
— Adult: 20 mg IO (1 mL of 2% lidocaine)
— Pediatric: 0.5 mg/kg IO; maximum 20 mg

sections.title: External Jugular (EJ) IV Access
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-23: Vascular Access — External Jugular
points: Paramedic
Site: midway between the angle of the jaw and the middle of the clavicle along the external jugular vein
Position: Trendelenburg (head down) to distend vein; turn head AWAY from insertion side
Insert bevel up at approximately 45-degree angle; advance smoothly
Apply light pressure just above the clavicle to distend vein before puncture
Use standard peripheral IV catheter — secure well with tape and dressing

sections.title: IV Fluid Administration
points: Authorized solutions: Normal Saline 0.9% (NS) or Lactated Ringer's (LR)
Normal Saline preferred for dilution and reconstitution of medications
FLUID BOLUS — ADULT: 1 liter wide open; may repeat ONCE; maximum 2 liters total
FLUID BOLUS — PEDIATRIC: 20 mL/kg wide open; may repeat to maximum 40 mL/kg total

MEDICATION COMPATIBILITY WITH LR: if giving a medication incompatible with LR (e.g., diazepam, phenytoin): pause LR infusion, flush line with 5–10 mL NS, administer medication, flush again with NS, then resume LR

key_drugs.name: Lidocaine 2% IO (Conscious IO Analgesia)
key_drugs.dose: Adult: 20 mg IO (1 mL of 2% lidocaine); pediatric: 0.5 mg/kg IO max 20 mg
key_drugs.indication: Reduce pain of IO fluid infusion in conscious patients
key_drugs.contraindications: Lidocaine allergy; do not use for cardiac dysrhythmia indications at this dose
key_drugs.side_effects: Local analgesia at IO site; systemic effects unlikely at this dose
key_drugs.notes: Slow IO push. IO analgesia dose — different from antidysrhythmic dosing.

deterioration_flags: 4 failed IV attempts — contact Medical Control; proceed with IO access if not already established
2 failed IO attempts — contact Medical Control before further IO attempts
Fluid bolus > 2L (adult) or > 40 mL/kg (pediatric) needed — contact Medical Control
Access needed in cardiac arrest adult — proximal humerus IO is preferred site for cardiac arrest to ensure medication delivery to central circulation
scope_notes: Specialist and Paramedic for IV/IO/EJ access
Do NOT access PICC, central lines, or AV fistulas without MC authorization
Max IV attempts: 2 per provider; 4 total
IO: consider early; indications include cardiac arrest, severe burns, shock, multi-system trauma with shock
Adult preferred IO site in cardiac arrest: proximal humerus
Pediatric IO (< 6 yr): proximal tibia preferred
After 2 failed IO attempts: contact MC
Lidocaine IO: adult 20 mg; peds 0.5 mg/kg max 20 mg for conscious patients
Fluids: NS or LR; adult bolus 1L x2 max 2L; peds 20 mL/kg x2 max 40 mL/kg
EJ: Paramedic only; Trendelenburg; head turned away from insertion side
